countermeasures.description: Collecting authentication events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authentication events are consistent with the baseline profile.

countermeasures.name: Authorization Event Thresholding
countermeasures.description: Collecting authorization events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authorization events are consistent with the baseline profile.

countermeasures.name: Credential Compromise Scope Analysis
countermeasures.description: Determining which credentials may have been compromised by analyzing the user logon history of a particular system.

countermeasures.name: Domain Account Monitoring
countermeasures.description: Monitoring the existence of or changes to Domain User Accounts.

countermeasures.name: Job Function Access Pattern Analysis
countermeasures.description: Detecting anomalies in user access patterns by comparing user access activity to behavioral profiles that categorize users by role such as job title, function, department.

countermeasures.name: Local Account Monitoring
countermeasures.description: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.

countermeasures.name: Resource Access Pattern Analysis
countermeasures.description: Analyzing the resources accessed by a user to identify unauthorized activity.

countermeasures.name: Session Duration Analysis
countermeasures.description: Analyzing the duration of user sessions in order to detect unauthorized  activity.

countermeasures.name: User Data Transfer Analysis
countermeasures.description: Analyzing the amount of data transferred by a user.

countermeasures.name: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
countermeasures.description: Monitoring geolocation data of user logon attempts and comparing it to a baseline user behavior profile to identify anomalies in logon location.

countermeasures.name: Web Session Activity Analysis
countermeasures.description: Monitoring changes in user web session behavior by comparing current web session activity to a baseline behavior profile or a catalog of predetermined malicious behavior.

countermeasures.name: File Analysis
countermeasures.description: File Analysis is an analytic process to determine a file's status. For example: virus, trojan, benign, malicious, trusted, unauthorized, sensitive, etc.

countermeasures.name: Dynamic Analysis
countermeasures.description: Executing or opening a file in a synthetic "sandbox" environment to determine if the file is a malicious program or if the file exploits another program such as a document reader.

countermeasures.name: Emulated File Analysis
countermeasures.description: Emulating instructions in a file looking for specific patterns.

countermeasures.name: File Content Rules
countermeasures.description: Employing a pattern matching rule language to analyze files.

countermeasures.name: File Hashing
countermeasures.description: Employing file hash comparisons to detect known malware.

countermeasures.name: Network Isolation
countermeasures.description: Network Isolation techniques prevent network hosts from accessing non-essential system network resources.

countermeasures.name: Broadcast Domain Isolation
countermeasures.description: Broadcast isolation restricts the number of computers a host can contact on their LAN.

countermeasures.name: DNS Allowlisting
countermeasures.description: Permitting only approved domains and their subdomains to be resolved.

countermeasures.name: DNS Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking DNS Network Traffic based on criteria such as IP address, domain name, or DNS query type.

countermeasures.name: Forward Resolution Domain Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking a lookup based on the query's domain name value.

countermeasures.name: Hierarchical Domain Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking the resolution of any subdomain of a specified domain name.

countermeasures.name: Homoglyph Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking DNS queries that are deceptively similar to legitimate domain names.

countermeasures.name: Forward Resolution IP Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking a DNS lookup's answer's IP address value.

countermeasures.name: Reverse Resolution Domain Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking a reverse DNS lookup's answer's domain name value.

countermeasures.name: Reverse Resolution IP Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking a reverse lookup based on the query's IP address value.

countermeasures.name: Encrypted Tunnels
countermeasures.description: Encrypted encapsulation of routable network traffic.

countermeasures.name: Network Traffic Filtering
countermeasures.description: Restricting network traffic originating from any location.

countermeasures.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
countermeasures.description: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

countermeasures.name: Outbound Traffic Filtering
countermeasures.description: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.

countermeasures.name: Execution Isolation
countermeasures.description: Execution Isolation techniques prevent application processes from accessing non-essential system resources, such as memory, devices, or files.

countermeasures.name: Executable Allowlisting
countermeasures.description: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

countermeasures.name: Executable Denylisting
countermeasures.description: Blocking the execution of files on a host in accordance with defined application policy rules.

countermeasures.name: Hardware-based Process Isolation
countermeasures.description: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.

countermeasures.name: IO Port Restriction
countermeasures.description: Limiting access to computer input/output (IO) ports to restrict unauthorized devices.

countermeasures.name: Kernel-based Process Isolation
countermeasures.description: Using kernel-level capabilities to isolate processes.

countermeasures.name: Mandatory Access Control
countermeasures.description: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

countermeasures.name: System Call Filtering
countermeasures.description: Configuring a kernel to use an allow or deny list to filter kernel api calls.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Environment
countermeasures.description: A Decoy Environment comprises hosts and networks for the purposes of deceiving an attacker.

countermeasures.name: Connected Honeynet
countermeasures.description: A decoy service, system, or environment, that is connected to the enterprise network, and simulates or emulates certain functionality to the network, without exposing full access to a production system.

countermeasures.name: Integrated Honeynet
countermeasures.description: The practice of setting decoys in a production environment to entice interaction from attackers.

countermeasures.name: Standalone Honeynet
countermeasures.description: An environment created for the purpose of attracting attackers and eliciting their behaviors that is not connected to any production enterprise systems.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Object
countermeasures.description: A Decoy Object is created and deployed for the purposes of deceiving attackers.

countermeasures.name: Decoy File
countermeasures.description: A file created for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Network Resource
countermeasures.description: Deploying a network resource for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Persona
countermeasures.description: Establishing a fake online identity to misdirect, deceive, and or interact with adversaries.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Public Release
countermeasures.description: Issuing publicly released media to deceive adversaries.

countermeasures.name: Decoy Session Token
countermeasures.description: An authentication token created for the purposes of deceiving an adversary.

countermeasures.name: Decoy User Credential
countermeasures.description: A Credential created for the purpose of deceiving an adversary.

countermeasures.name: File Eviction
countermeasures.description: File eviction techniques evict files from system storage.

countermeasures.name: File Removal
countermeasures.description: The file removal technique deletes malicious artifacts or programs from a computer system.

countermeasures.name: Email Removal
countermeasures.description: The email removal technique deletes email files from system storage.

countermeasures.name: Credential Eviction
countermeasures.description: Credential Eviction techniques disable or remove compromised credentials from a computer network.

countermeasures.name: Account Locking
countermeasures.description: The process of temporarily disabling user accounts on a system or domain.

countermeasures.name: Authentication Cache Invalidation
countermeasures.description: Removing tokens or credentials from an authentication cache to prevent further user associated account accesses.

countermeasures.name: Credential Revoking
countermeasures.description: Deleting a set of credentials permanently to prevent them from being used to authenticate.

countermeasures.name: Process Eviction
countermeasures.description: Process eviction techniques terminate or remove running process.

countermeasures.name: Process Termination
countermeasures.description: Terminating a running application process on a computer system.

countermeasures.name: Process Suspension
countermeasures.description: Suspending a running process on a computer system.
